title: pimelea pygmaea
intro: Pimelea pygmaea, also known as dwarf rice flower, is a small shrub native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pimelea pygmaea include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing, making it a popular choice for topical applications. Pimelea pygmaea can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
